ddls:
CREATE TABLE "interest_rates" (
  "interest_rate_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each interest rate */,
  "loan_type" TEXT /* Type of loan (e.g., personal, business, car) */,
  "interest_rate" REAL /* Interest rate for the loan type */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("interest_rate_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "loan_investors" (
  "loan_investor_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each loan investor */,
  "loan_id" INTEGER /* ID of the loan being invested in */,
  "investor_id" INTEGER /* ID of the investor */,
  "investment_amount" REAL /* Amount invested by the investor */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("loan_investor_id")
)
column_names: Unique identifier for each interest rate
Type of loan (e.g., personal, business, car)
Interest rate for the loan type
Unique identifier for each loan investor
ID of the loan being invested in
